reason: The statement applies because the text describes a series of plagues sent by Yahweh to Egypt through Moses and Aaron to convince Pharaoh to release the Hebrews. These plagues include turning the water into blood, frogs, lice, flies, cattle disease, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, and the death of the firstborn. These events demonstrate Yahweh's power and intervention on behalf of the Hebrews, fulfilling the idea of a divine figure acting to save the Hebrews.
